I agree. The ampulla provides not only the greatest space and nutrient availability but also allows for longer incubation periods before rupture, giving clinicians more time to intervene surgically—this extended window significantly improves patient outcomes compared to smaller, less accessible sections like the interstitial or isthmic regions. Answer: B
